chunk_summary: Guidelines for vitamin A, zinc, and other micronutrient supplementation in Indian infants and young children, focusing on prophylaxis, therapeutic use, and counseling for deficiencies through 24 months.
learning_objectives: Understand the vitamin A supplementation schedule and therapeutic use in Indian infants and young children.
Learn zinc supplementation protocols for acute diarrhea in children under 24 months.
Identify risk factors and counseling strategies for other micronutrient deficiencies, including iodine and vitamin B12.
Recognize the importance of documenting doses to prevent missed or duplicated administration.
key_facts: Vitamin A prophylaxis: 100,000 IU at 9 months, 200,000 IU at 18 months, and every 6 months thereafter.
Therapeutic vitamin A: 100,000 IU (<12 months) or 200,000 IU (≥12 months) for measles or severe acute malnutrition.
Zinc for acute diarrhea: 10 mg/day (<6 months) or 20 mg/day (≥6 months) for 14 days with ORS.
Iodine: Ensure household use of iodized salt; breastfed infants depend on maternal iodine intake.
Vitamin B12: Risk higher in infants of mothers on strict vegetarian diets; evaluate for developmental regression or anemia.
reference_quote: Vitamin A prophylaxis in India begins in late infancy and supports vision and immune function. The routine program dose is IU 100,000 at 9 months (often with measles-containing vaccine), followed by IU 200,000 at 18 months and then every 6 months thereafter.
document_key_topics: Vitamin A supplementation and therapeutic use
Counseling for iodine and vitamin B12 deficiencies
